_national.name: University Emergency Hardship Funds
_national.description: Most US universities have emergency hardship funds for students in financial crisis. Contact your Dean of Students office or financial aid office — do not assume it doesn't exist. These funds are often underutilized because students don't ask.
_national.action: Email your Dean of Students: 'I am an international student facing an unexpected medical bill of $X. Do you have emergency hardship funds I can apply for?'

_national.name: International Student Services Office
_national.description: Your university's international student office (often called ISSS or OIS) may know of scholarships, emergency funds, and local resources specifically for international students facing medical bills.
_national.action: Schedule a meeting with your DSO (Designated School Official) and ask explicitly about emergency financial resources.

_national.name: Student Health Services
_national.description: Many campus health centers can help you navigate off-campus medical bills, connect you with patient advocates, or provide documentation of your student status for hospital financial assistance applications.
_national.action: Contact your campus student health services and ask if they have a patient advocate or financial counselor.

_national.name: Dollar For — Free Charity Care Application Help
_national.description: Dollar For is a nonprofit that will personally help you apply for hospital charity care. Free. They specialize in helping patients who don't know how to navigate the process.
_national.action: Go to dollarfor.org and start an application. They will guide you through the process.
